entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: The crop species Cicer arietinum L. studied for salinity response.

entities.name: SALINITY
entities.description: The environmental stress condition affecting the growth and yield of the crop.

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The agricultural productivity measure of the chickpea RILs.

entities.name: PHYSIOLOGICAL PHENOTYPING
entities.description: The process used to identify salt tolerant recombinant inbred lines.

entities.name: RECOMBINANT INBRED LINES
entities.description: A population of 232 F8 lines derived from ICCV 10 x DCP 92-3 used in the study.

entities.name: RELATIVE WATER CONTENT
entities.description: A physiological measure that decreased under high salt stress.

entities.name: CHLOROPHYLL CONTENT
entities.description: A physiological measure that decreased under high salt stress.

entities.name: SODIUM ACCUMULATION
entities.description: The accumulation of Na+ ions in the shoot, where lower levels indicate tolerance.

entities.name: POTASSIUM ACCUMULATION
entities.description: The accumulation of K+ ions in the shoot, where higher levels indicate tolerance.

entities.name: LEAF PROLINE CONTENT
entities.description: A biochemical marker that increases in tolerant genotypes under stress.

entities.name: ION LEAKAGE
entities.description: The leakage of ions from cells, where less leakage indicates tolerance.

entities.name: SALT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of the plant to maintain growth and yield under salinity stress.
